title: The Robot Brains: OpenAI's Ilya Sutskever
excerpts:
Section Title: The Robot Brains: OpenAI's Ilya Sutskever
Content:
The Industry
September 21, 2021
Pieter Abbeel
Co-Founder & Chief Scientist
Share
[Share to Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=) [Share to Twitter](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=) Share via Email [Share to LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/sharing/share-offsite/?url=)
Copied!
Copy Link
The Robot Brains: OpenAI's Ilya Sutskever
On the last episode of Season One, our guest is [Ilya Sutskever](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ilya_Sutskever) . Ilya is the Co-Founder and Chief Scientist of [OpenAI](https://openai.com/) . As a PhD student at Toronto, Ilya was one of the authors on the [2012 AlexNet paper](https://papers.nips.cc/paper/2012/hash/c399862d3b9d6b76c8436e924a68c45b-Abstract.html) that completely changed the field of AI, resulting in the widespread adoption of deep learning and the avalanche of AI breakthroughs we’ve seen the past 10 years.

title: Ilya Sutskever's home page
excerpts:
**Ilya Sutskever**
Co-founder and Chief Scientist of [OpenAI](http://www.openai.com) .
I spent three wonderful years as a Research Scientist at the Google Brain Team.
Before that, I was a co-founder of [DNNresearch](http://www.dnnresearch.com) .
And before that, I was a postdoc in Stanford with [Andrew Ng](http://www.cs.stanford.edu/~ang) 's group.
And in the beginning, I was a student in the Machine Learning group of Toronto, working with Geoffrey Hinton .

title: Ilya Sutskever
excerpts: **Ilya Sutskever** ( Hebrew : איליה סוצקבר ; born 1986) is a computer scientist who specializes in machine learning . <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup> He has made several major contributions to the field of deep learning , including sequence-to-sequence learning , reasoning models , GPT models, and contributions to CLIP , DALL-E , and AlphaGo . <sup>[[ 5 ]](:0-5)</sup> With Alex Krizhevsky and Geoffrey Hinton , he co-created AlexNet , a convolutional neural network . <sup>[[ 6 ]]()</sup> One of the most highly cited computer scientists in history, he has won the NeurIPS Test of Time Award for his lasting impact on AI research three times in a row (2022-2024) and received the National Academy of Sciences Award for the Industrial Application of Science in 2026. <sup>[[ 7 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup>

title: Ilya Sutskever | Computer Scientist | Bio | Open AI and SSI
excerpts:
Section Title: Ilya Sutskever > Co-founding OpenAI and developing ChatGPT
Content:
His research has also encompassed areas such as generative models, reinforcement learning, and unsupervised learning, contributing to a broader understanding of how machines can learn from data.
Ilya Sutskever was a pivotal figure at OpenAI, serving as one of its co-founders and its Chief Scientist. His technical contributions to the development of models like ChatGPT were rooted in his work on deep neural networks and sequence modeling. Early in his career, Sutskever co-authored influential papers on sequence-to-sequence learning—most notably the 2014 paper "Sequence to Sequence Learning with Neural Networks"—which demonstrated that recurrent neural networks (RNNs), particularly those enhanced with Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) units, could effectively model and generate sequential data.
